Braem Building

Registrations are open

Click here for all registration information

ECOOP 2026

ECOOP is Europe’s longest-standing annual Programming Languages conference, bringing together researchers, practitioners, and students to share their ideas and experiences in all topics related to programming languages, software development, systems and applications. ECOOP welcomes high quality research papers relating to these fields in a broad sense.

ECOOP is committed to affordable open access publishing. Recent year’s publications have been published by Dagstuhl’s LIPIcs series under a Creative Commons CC-BY license where the authors retain their copyright. ECOOP articles have been published without open access publishing fee and can be accessed via a DOI. LIPIcs is indexed in DBLP, Google Scholar, Scopus and others.

In 2026, ECOOP will be hosted by the Software Languages Lab at the Vrije Universiteit Brussel in Brussels, Belgium.

Please visit the Technical Papers-track for the full Call for Papers!

ECOOP Keynote Speakers

Keynote Speaker
Building trust: a journey from mechanized semantics to verified compilation and beyond
Sandrine Blazy
AITO Dahl-Nygaard Junior Prize
Language Design and Implementation Where Paradigms Converge
Yizhou Zhang
AITO Dahl-Nygaard Senior Prize
15 Years of Viper: Building and Evolving a Verification Infrastructure
Peter Müller

ECOOP Academy Speakers

Programming Systems, or What Programming Language Research Cannot See
Tomas Petricek
The challenge of data sharing while respecting privacy
Alejandro Russo
Can WebAssembly Be the Universal Software Substrate?
Ben L. Titzer
Taming the Object Graph: What Types Can Guarantee
Sophia Drossopoulou
Getting Started with Quantum Software Engineering
Shaukat Ali
Modelling, Verifying & Implementing database backends
Marc Shapiro
Safe Autonomous Agents via Shielding
Bettina Könighofer
Benchmarking on Modern Hardware: Techniques for Performance Comparisons from Day-To-Day Experimenting to Paper Writing
Stefan Marr
Formal Methods Meet Legal Implementations
Raphaël Monat
